entities.name: vāyu
entities.cite: Air, set in motion by the effort that follows the speaker's wish, striking upon the places of articulation, attains the condition of the word

entities.name: śabdatva
entities.cite: air attains the condition of the word: sabdatvam pratipadyate

entities.name: icchā
entities.cite: the causal chain runs unbroken from mind to matter: iccha, the wish

entities.name: prayatna
entities.cite: effort conforming to it; air labdha-kriya, endowed with motion

entities.name: sthānāni
entities.cite: striking the eight classical places of articulation

entities.name: vega
entities.cite: this moving air carries vega and pracaya, velocity and mass-accumulation

entities.name: pracaya
entities.cite: possessing the properties of velocity and accumulation

entities.name: sāmarthya
entities.cite: Through the potency of its cause

entities.name: sannipāta
entities.cite: at full sannipata it splits even solid bodies

axioms.name: abhihati
axioms.cite: striking the eight classical places of articulation

axioms.name: yuktavega
axioms.cite: this moving air carries vega and pracaya, velocity and mass-accumulation, from the samarthya of its cause

axioms.name: yukti-pracaya
axioms.cite: carries vega and pracaya, velocity and mass-accumulation

axioms.name: vibhajyante
axioms.cite: at full sannipata it splits even solid bodies

denials.name: paryapta
denials.cite: air shaped at the lips has still to be *grasped as one word* by a hearer

claims.name: anuvartati
claims.cite: effort that follows the speaker's wish

claims.name: pratipadyate
claims.cite: attains the condition of the word

claims.name: abhihati
claims.cite: striking upon the places of articulation

claims.name: yukti-pracaya
claims.cite: possessing the properties of velocity and accumulation

claims.name: vibhajyante
claims.cite: divides by its impact even bodies that possess solidity

rejected_readings.label: vayu_is_sufficient
rejected_readings.rendering: Air alone, shaped by the speaker's will and striking the places of articulation, is fully the word itself, capable of being understood by any hearer.
rejected_readings.why: The verse presents the vayu doctrine as mechanist explanation of word-formation, but the commentary explicitly notes this is incomplete: shaped air must be grasped as one word by the hearer, which requires the epistemic unity that air-collision alone cannot provide. This reading treats the material mechanism as sufficient when Bhartrhari shows it is necessary but not sufficient.

rejected_readings.label: vayu_primary_cause
rejected_readings.rendering: The air itself is the primary cause of wordhood, generated by the speaker's wish without any intervening mechanism.
rejected_readings.why: The axioms establish a chain: iccha → prayatna → vayu's motion → collision with sthanani → sabdatva. This reading elides the causal stages and treats vayu as originating from wish directly, losing the mechanistic precision Bhartrhari is documenting. The chain is what the doctrine claims.

rejected_readings.label: sabdatva_achieved_passively
rejected_readings.rendering: Air naturally carries the property of wordhood without requiring the effort of the speaker to direct its motion.
rejected_readings.why: The verse is explicit: effort (prayatna) following the wish (iccha) is what sets the air in motion. Wordhood is achieved through this intentional, effortful shaping, not passively. This reading treats sabdatva as an inherent property rather than an attainment (pratipadyate), contradicting the causal structure.

notes: The verse presents the vayu doctrine (mechanism of word-formation through air shaped by intention and striking articulation points) in full empirical detail. The contract captures the causal chain: iccha→prayatna→vayu's motion→collision→sabdatva-pratti, and the physical robustness (vega, pracaya) that allows air to divide even solid bodies. The key tension Bhartrhari raises (without adopting the doctrine) is that material mechanism alone cannot explain how shaped air is *grasped* as one word by the hearer—that requires the unity-dimension that lives in the order documented in 1.82-92. Denial registers this incompleteness without overstating it; vayu-mechanism is real but not the whole story.
